title: Vriesea vagans
taxonomy: Vriesea vagans is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. This family is a group of monocotyledonous plants, which are characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. Vriesea vagans is also classified as a genus within the subfamily Tillandsioideae, which includes various species of air plants. The Bromeliaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Tillandsioideae subfamily. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Vriesea vagans and other plants within the family.

intro: Vriesea vagans, also known as the 'Vagans Air Plant', is a species of epiphytic bromeliad, a type of tropical plant that grows on other plants or surfaces. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, boasting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues.
